hypotheses.name: Pedestrian
hypotheses.description: Person on foot detected by sensors

hypotheses.name: Cyclist
hypotheses.description: Person on bicycle detected by sensors

evidence.description: Radar signature consistent with large metallic object moving at vehicle speed
Camera detects two-wheeled silhouette with upright rider posture

massAssignments.justification: Radar strongly supports Vehicle: reliability 0.85 × detection confidence 0.85
Residual ignorance — radar cannot fully distinguish at this range
Camera supports Cyclist or Pedestrian: reliability 0.7 × detection confidence 0.8
Residual ignorance — camera resolution insufficient for definitive classification
Dempster's rule normalizing conflict between radar (Vehicle) and camera (Cyclist/Pedestrian)
Combined mass for non-vehicle hypotheses after normalization
Remaining ignorance after combining both sources

decisions.name: Object classification decision
decisions.reasoning: Vehicle has the highest belief (0.42) and plausibility (0.72). Despite camera suggesting Cyclist/Pedestrian, radar reliability advantage (0.85 vs 0.70) dominates. High conflict mass (0.40) signals the sensors fundamentally disagree — additional sensors recommended.
